Systems Serology — WT_IgG Effector Pathway Assessment

IgG1 → FcγR → ADCD → WT_IgG · six-step logic check · maternal-arm stratified · 8-week visit

Scot Zens and Anja Saso

2026-07-05

WT_IgG Effector-Pathway Assessment

This document tests, link by link, whether the hypothesised effector pathway

\[\text{antigen IgG1/IgG3} \;\rightarrow\; \text{Fc}\gamma\text{R binding (Fc}\gamma\text{R2a, Fc}\gamma\text{R3b)} \;\rightarrow\; \text{ADCD} \;\rightarrow\; \textbf{WT_IgG}\]

is structurally supported at the infant 8-week timepoint (InfMon2), and whether support holds in both maternal arms (TdaP vs TT). The original concurrent analysis regressed each feature class on the outcome in parallel; these six steps instead probe the edges between the intermediate nodes that a competing-predictor design cannot see.

Antigens carried through every step: PT, FHA, PRN (focal chain: PRN). Each step is a self-contained child; run them in order.

  1. Full pathway network by arm — one GGM per antigen containing IgG1/IgG3, both FcγRs, ADCD and the outcome, so the chain-adjacent edges become visible.
  2. Explicit link regressions with an arm interaction — the two intermediate edges (IgG→FcγR; FcγR→ADCD) fitted directly, with a maternal-arm interaction to test whether link strength differs by arm.
  3. Serial mediation by arm — a path model quantifying the IgG1 → FcγR → ADCD → outcome indirect effect.
  4. Variance decomposition by arm — does ADCD (and FcγR) add unique variance beyond IgG1, or is it downstream?
  5. FcγR collinearity diagnostics — recover the FcγR3b-specific signal that partial-correlation networks zero out.
  6. Pathway-network NCT by arm — the arm comparison run on the FcγR+ADCD and joint-pathway networks that step-5 of the prior analysis skipped.
  7. FcγR3b unique role — separate the two collinear receptors (residual-of-3b-on-2a, and the 2a/3b ratio) and test whether FcγR3b independently predicts ADCD and the outcome.
  8. Aggregate complement vs whole-cell outcome — a summed antigen-specific ADCD score against the whole-cell WT_IgG, and PRN-ADCD with quantity removed.
  9. Arm × FcγR interaction tests — a formal test of whether the FcγR→outcome mapping differs by maternal arm (different wiring) rather than only in strength (different forcing).
  10. FcγR3b vs ADCD for the outcome — whether FcγR3b’s link to killing survives adjustment for ADCD, adjudicating the ADCD/outcome discordance.
  11. Effector activation by FcγR — whether each effector function (ADCD, ADCP, ADNP) is engaged by FcγR2a/FcγR3b, per antigen and arm, with the FcγR3b-specific contribution and an arm interaction.
  12. Each effector on the path to the outcome — whether ADCP and ADNP (like ADCD) add to WT_IgG beyond antibody quantity and beyond FcγR, and whether each survives jointly with FcγR3b.
  13. Antigen-specificity and multi-effector model — focal PRN vs aggregate (mean-across-antigens) effector scores, and a joint ADCD+ADCP+ADNP model, to see whether any effector is independently on the path and whether the association is antigen-specific or broad.

Steps 3, 4, 7, 8, 9 and 10 run in every available data mode: raw (quantity), igg_std (all predictors residualized on total IgG — quality), and mixed (IgG1/IgG3 raw as the quantity exposure, FcγR & ADCD residualized as quality mediators, outcome raw). The mixed mode most directly tests “antibody amount flowing through per-antibody engagement efficiency to killing.” Available modes here: raw, igg_std, mixed (igg_std/mixed require the residual files).

Interpretation is cross-sectional: these steps establish whether the pathway’s footprint is present and internally consistent, not causal direction. All models are complete-case; cells below 30 are skipped and logged.

Step 1 — Full Pathway Network by Arm

For each antigen and each maternal arm, a single Gaussian graphical model (EBICglasso, partial correlations) is fit on the chain nodes IgG1, IgG3, FcγR2a, FcγR3b, ADCD together with WT_IgG. Because all four node types sit in one graph, the edges the parallel-regression design could not show — IgG→FcγR, FcγR→ADCD, ADCD→outcome — are now directly readable. The chain-edge table isolates exactly those hypothesised adjacencies.

.stage(1, "full pathway network by arm")
stopifnot(exists("path_wide"))

# partial-correlation edge between two nodes, read off the estimated graph
edge_of <- function(g, nodes, a, b) if (all(c(a, b) %in% nodes)) g[a, b] else NA_real_

chain_edges_tbl <- function(g, nodes, A) {
  o <- OUTCOME
  rows <- list(
    c(paste0(A, "_IgG1"),   paste0(A, "_FcgR2a")),
    c(paste0(A, "_IgG1"),   paste0(A, "_FcgR3b")),
    c(paste0(A, "_IgG3"),   paste0(A, "_FcgR2a")),
    c(paste0(A, "_IgG3"),   paste0(A, "_FcgR3b")),
    c(paste0(A, "_FcgR2a"), paste0(A, "_ADCD")),
    c(paste0(A, "_FcgR3b"), paste0(A, "_ADCD")),
    c(paste0(A, "_ADCD"),   o),
    c(paste0(A, "_FcgR2a"), o),
    c(paste0(A, "_FcgR3b"), o),
    c(paste0(A, "_IgG1"),   o))
  data.frame(
    link = vapply(rows, function(r) paste(sub(paste0("^", A, "_"), "", r[1]),
                                          sub(paste0("^", A, "_"), "", sub(OUTCOME, "OUTCOME", r[2])),
                                          sep = " — "), character(1)),
    partial_r = vapply(rows, function(r) round(edge_of(g, nodes, r[1], r[2]), 3), numeric(1)),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}

if (!have_net) {
  cat("*Network packages not installed; step 1 skipped.*\n\n")
} else {
  for (A in PATHWAY_ANTIGENS) {
    cat("## ", A, " pathway\n\n", sep = "")
    for (m in cfg$maternal_arms) {
      nodes <- c(chain_feats(A), OUTCOME)
      d <- arm_data(m) %>% dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(nodes)) %>% tidyr::drop_na()
      nt_log(nt, "Pathway", paste0("Step1 net: ", A), paste0(A, " @ ", CONC_VISIT), m, nodes, arm_data(m))
      cat("### ", m, " mothers (n = ", nrow(d), ")\n\n", sep = "")
      .info("step1 %s %s network n=%d", A, m, nrow(d))
      if (nrow(d) < NETWORK_MIN) { cat(sprintf("*n < %d; skipped.*\n\n", NETWORK_MIN)); next }
      net <- tryCatch(bootnet::estimateNetwork(d, default = "EBICglasso", threshold = TRUE),
                      error = function(e) NULL)
      if (is.null(net)) { cat("*Estimation failed.*\n\n"); next }
      short <- sub(paste0("^", A, "_"), "", nodes); short[nodes == OUTCOME] <- "OUTCOME"
      grp <- list(IgG  = which(nodes %in% paste0(A, c("_IgG1", "_IgG3"))),
                  FcgR = which(nodes %in% paste0(A, c("_FcgR2a", "_FcgR3b"))),
                  ADCD = which(nodes == paste0(A, "_ADCD")),
                  OUT  = which(nodes == OUTCOME))
      qgraph::qgraph(net$graph, groups = grp, color = c("steelblue", "darkorange", "purple", "gold"),
                     labels = short, layout = "spring", vsize = 11, legend = TRUE,
                     legend.cex = 0.35, title = paste0(A, " — ", m))
      print(knitr::kable(chain_edges_tbl(net$graph, nodes, A),
                         caption = paste0("Chain-adjacent partial correlations — ", A, ", ", m,
                                          " (0 = no edge after conditioning)."),
                         col.names = c("Link", "Partial r"))); cat("\n")
    }
  }
}

Link Partial r
IgG1 — FcgR2a 0.738
IgG1 — FcgR3b 0.000
IgG3 — FcgR2a 0.000
IgG3 — FcgR3b 0.000
FcgR2a — ADCD 0.231
FcgR3b — ADCD 0.000
ADCD — OUTCOME 0.000
FcgR2a — OUTCOME 0.000
FcgR3b — OUTCOME 0.000
IgG1 — OUTCOME 0.219

How to read this. A non-zero IgG1 — FcgR3b edge is direct evidence of co-regulation; a non-zero FcgR3b — ADCD edge is evidence for the activation step; a non-zero ADCD — OUTCOME edge places ADCD adjacent to killing. If a chain edge is 0 while a “shortcut” edge (e.g. IgG1 — OUTCOME) is large, the pathway is not mediating and the logic needs revisiting. Compare the two arms row by row: similar signs/positions = intact in both; a link present in one arm only = arm-specific pathway.

Step 2 — Explicit Link Regressions with Arm Interaction

The two intermediate edges are fitted directly, rather than inferred from parallel outcome regressions:

  • Link A (co-regulation): FcγR ~ IgG1 + IgG3 — does subclass predict receptor binding?
  • Link B (activation): ADCD ~ FcγR2a + FcγR3b — does receptor binding predict complement deposition?

Each is fit per arm, then on the pooled data with a maternal-arm interaction so the slope difference (TdaP vs TT) is an explicit coefficient and p-value — the “different strengths in the two groups” question, tested rather than eyeballed. The reference arm is TdaP.

.stage(2, "explicit link regressions with arm interaction")
for (A in PATHWAY_ANTIGENS) {
  cat("## ", A, "\n\n", sep = "")
  ig  <- keep_present(paste0(A, c("_IgG1", "_IgG3")))
  fcg <- keep_present(paste0(A, c("_FcgR2a", "_FcgR3b")))
  # Link A: each receptor on subclasses
  for (rc in fcg) if (length(ig)) link_report(rc, ig, paste0("Link A — ", rc, " ~ IgG1 + IgG3"))
  # Link B: ADCD on both receptors
  adcd <- paste0(A, "_ADCD")
  if (adcd %in% names(path_wide) && length(fcg))
    link_report(adcd, fcg, paste0("Link B — ", A, "_ADCD ~ FcγR2a + FcγR3b"))
}

term beta se t p sig

PRN_FcgR3b × TT 0.258 0.134 1.92 0.056 ns

How to read this. A significant positive Link-A slope on IgG1 (especially for FcγR3b) supports IgG1↔︎FcγR co-regulation; a significant Link-B slope supports FcγR-dependent ADCD. A significant × arm interaction means that link is genuinely stronger in one arm; a non-significant interaction means the link operates at similar strength in both, even if overall signal differs — which would argue the model is intact in both arms and only the inputs differ.

Step 3 — Serial Mediation by Arm

A path model quantifying the serial indirect effect IgG1 → FcγR → ADCD → WT_IgG, fit separately in each maternal arm. This is the operational test of “the pathway is intact”: if the serial indirect through FcγR3b is significant and the residual direct IgG1→outcome path is small, the chain is carrying the signal. Standardized (std.all) solutions are shown so paths are comparable across arms and antigens. The model is fit in each available data mode: raw (quantity-dominated) and, when residual files are present, igg_std (predictors residualized on total IgG, outcome kept raw) — the latter being the fair test of whether FcγR/ADCD mediate the outcome independently of antibody quantity.

.stage(3, "serial mediation by arm")
med_model <- '
  fcgr2a ~ a1*igg1 + igg3
  fcgr3b ~ a2*igg1 + igg3
  adcd   ~ b1*fcgr2a + b2*fcgr3b + d*igg1
  y      ~ g*adcd + h1*fcgr2a + h2*fcgr3b + k*igg1
  ind_3b := a2*b2*g
  ind_2a := a1*b1*g
  direct := k
'
if (!RUN_MEDIATION || !have_lavaan) {
  cat("*Step 3 skipped (", if (!have_lavaan) "lavaan not installed" else "run_mediation = FALSE", ").*\n\n", sep = "")
} else {
  .modes <- if (RUN_STD_RERUNS) MODES else "raw"
  for (mode in .modes) {
    cat("## Data mode: `", mode, "`", mode_desc(mode), "\n\n", sep = "")
    wd <- wide_for(mode)
    for (A in PATHWAY_ANTIGENS) {
      need <- paste0(A, c("_IgG1", "_IgG3", "_FcgR2a", "_FcgR3b", "_ADCD"))
      if (!all(c(need, OUTCOME) %in% names(wd))) { cat("### ", A, "\n\n*Missing nodes; skipped.*\n\n", sep = ""); next }
      cat("### ", A, "\n\n", sep = "")
      for (m in cfg$maternal_arms) {
        d <- arm_data_m(mode, m) %>% dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(c(need, OUTCOME))) %>% tidyr::drop_na()
        colnames(d) <- c("igg1", "igg3", "fcgr2a", "fcgr3b", "adcd", "y")
        d <- as.data.frame(scale(d))
        nt_log(nt, "Pathway", paste0("Step3 mediation [", mode, "]: ", A), A, m, need, arm_data_m(mode, m))
        cat("#### ", m, " mothers (n = ", nrow(d), ")\n\n", sep = "")
        .info("step3 [%s] %s %s mediation n=%d", mode, A, m, nrow(d))
        if (nrow(d) < NETWORK_MIN) { cat(sprintf("*n < %d; skipped.*\n\n", NETWORK_MIN)); next }
        fit <- tryCatch(lavaan::sem(med_model, data = d, fixed.x = FALSE), error = function(e) NULL)
        if (is.null(fit) || !lavaan::lavInspect(fit, "converged")) {
          cat("*Model did not converge.*\n\n"); next }
        ss <- lavaan::standardizedSolution(fit)
        key <- ss[ss$label %in% c("a2", "b2", "g", "k", "ind_3b", "ind_2a", "direct"),
                   c("label", "est.std", "se", "z", "pvalue")]
        names(key) <- c("path", "std_est", "se", "z", "p")
        key$path <- c(a2 = "IgG1→FcγR3b (a2)", b2 = "FcγR3b→ADCD (b2)", g = "ADCD→OUTCOME (g)",
                      k = "IgG1→OUTCOME direct (k)", ind_3b = "serial via FcγR3b (a2·b2·g)",
                      ind_2a = "serial via FcγR2a (a1·b1·g)", direct = "direct (k)")[key$path]
        key[, c("std_est", "se", "z", "p")] <- lapply(key[, c("std_est", "se", "z", "p")],
                                                       function(x) signif(x, 3))
        print(knitr::kable(key, row.names = FALSE,
                           caption = paste0("Standardized key paths — ", A, ", ", m, " [", mode, "]."))); cat("\n")
      }
    }
  }
}

Data mode: raw — untransformed log10 (quantity)

PT

TdaP mothers (n = 112)

Standardized key paths — PT, TdaP [raw].
path std_est se z p
IgG1→FcγR3b (a2) 0.7410 0.0429 17.300 0.0000
FcγR3b→ADCD (b2) -0.5420 0.0992 -5.470 0.0000
ADCD→OUTCOME (g) -0.0779 0.1300 -0.600 0.5490
IgG1→OUTCOME direct (k) 0.3120 0.1850 1.680 0.0921

TT mothers (n = 109)

Standardized key paths — PRN, TT [mixed].
path std_est se z p
IgG1→FcγR3b (a2) -0.206000 0.10100 -2.040 4.14e-02
FcγR3b→ADCD (b2) -0.088600 0.08430 -1.050 2.93e-01
ADCD→OUTCOME (g) -0.020500 0.09900 -0.207 8.36e-01
IgG1→OUTCOME direct (k) 0.408000 0.08050 5.070 4.00e-07
serial via FcγR3b (a2·b2·g) -0.000374 0.00185 -0.202 8.40e-01
serial via FcγR2a (a1·b1·g) -0.000587 0.00302 -0.195 8.46e-01
direct (k) 0.408000 0.08050 5.070 4.00e-07

How to read this. A significant serial via FcγR3b (a2·b2·g) indirect effect is direct support that the FcγR3b-specific chain transmits antibody quantity to killing. If the direct (k) path shrinks toward zero once the chain is in the model, mediation is (near-)complete; a large surviving direct path means routes outside ADCD also matter. Comparing arms, similar indirect magnitudes = the mechanism operates in both; a collapsed indirect in one arm = a broken link there.

Step 4 — Variance Decomposition by Arm

For each antigen and arm, WT_IgG is modelled by nested layers of the chain and the incremental variance is measured:

  • M1: outcome ~ IgG1
  • M2: + FcγR2a + FcγR3b
  • M3: + ADCD

The key question: does ADCD add unique variance beyond IgG1 + FcγR, or has the FcγR layer already captured it? If ADCD’s incremental R² is ~0, ADCD behaves as a downstream mediator (consistent with the pathway); if it retains unique variance, it is a partially independent complement arm. relaimpo LMG shares (averaged over orderings) show each term’s unique portion in the full model.

.stage(4, "variance decomposition by arm")
delta_row <- function(m0, m1, lab) {
  a <- anova(m0, m1)
  data.frame(step = lab,
             R2 = round(summary(m1)$r.squared, 3),
             dR2 = round(summary(m1)$r.squared - summary(m0)$r.squared, 3),
             F = round(a$F[2], 2), df = paste0(a$Df[2], ",", a$Res.Df[2]),
             p = signif(a$`Pr(>F)`[2], 3), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}
.modes <- if (RUN_STD_RERUNS) MODES else "raw"
for (mode in .modes) {
  cat("# Data mode: `", mode, "`", mode_desc(mode), "\n\n", sep = "")
  wd <- wide_for(mode)
  for (A in PATHWAY_ANTIGENS) {
    cat("## ", A, "\n\n", sep = "")
    igg1 <- paste0(A, "_IgG1"); fcg <- intersect(paste0(A, c("_FcgR2a", "_FcgR3b")), names(wd))
    adcd <- paste0(A, "_ADCD")
    vars <- intersect(c(igg1, fcg, adcd), names(wd))
    if (!(igg1 %in% names(wd)) || !length(fcg) || !(adcd %in% names(wd)) || !(OUTCOME %in% names(wd))) {
      cat("*Incomplete chain; skipped.*\n\n"); next }
    for (m in cfg$maternal_arms) {
      d <- arm_data_m(mode, m) %>% dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(c(OUTCOME, vars))) %>% tidyr::drop_na()
      cat("### ", m, " mothers (n = ", nrow(d), ")\n\n", sep = "")
      .info("step4 [%s] %s %s n=%d", mode, A, m, nrow(d))
      if (nrow(d) < NETWORK_MIN) { cat(sprintf("*n < %d; skipped.*\n\n", NETWORK_MIN)); next }
      m1 <- lm(reformulate(igg1,          response = OUTCOME), data = d)
      m2 <- lm(reformulate(c(igg1, fcg),  response = OUTCOME), data = d)
      m3 <- lm(reformulate(vars,          response = OUTCOME), data = d)
      tab <- rbind(
        data.frame(step = "M1: IgG1", R2 = round(summary(m1)$r.squared, 3),
                   dR2 = round(summary(m1)$r.squared, 3), F = NA, df = "", p = NA),
        delta_row(m1, m2, "M2: + FcγR2a/3b"),
        delta_row(m2, m3, "M3: + ADCD"))
      print(knitr::kable(tab, row.names = FALSE,
                         caption = paste0("Incremental variance — ", A, ", ", m, " [", mode, "]."))); cat("\n")
      if (have_relimp && nrow(d) > length(vars) + 2) {
        ri <- tryCatch(relaimpo::calc.relimp(m3, type = "lmg", rela = FALSE), error = function(e) NULL)
        if (!is.null(ri)) {
          lm_tab <- data.frame(term = names(ri@lmg), unique_R2_lmg = round(ri@lmg, 3),
                               row.names = NULL)
          lm_tab <- lm_tab[order(-lm_tab$unique_R2_lmg), ]
          print(knitr::kable(lm_tab, row.names = FALSE,
                             caption = paste0("LMG unique-variance shares (full M3) — ", A, ", ", m, " [", mode, "]."))); cat("\n")
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Data mode: raw — untransformed log10 (quantity)

PT_ADCD 0.018
PT_FcgR3b 0.007
PT_FcgR2a 0.001

TT mothers (n = 109)

Incremental variance — PT, TT [mixed].
step R2 dR2 F df p
M1: IgG1 0.076 0.076 NA NA
M2: + FcγR2a/3b 0.078 0.002 0.11 2,105 0.895
M3: + ADCD 0.078 0.000 0.01 1,104 0.921
LMG unique-variance shares (full M3) — PT, TT [mixed].
term unique_R2_lmg
PT_IgG1 0.066
PT_ADCD 0.009
PT_FcgR3b 0.002
PT_FcgR2a 0.001

FHA

TdaP mothers (n = 112)

Incremental variance — FHA, TdaP [mixed].
step R2 dR2 F df p
M1: IgG1 0.033 0.033 NA NA
M2: + FcγR2a/3b 0.036 0.003 0.16 2,108 0.85
M3: + ADCD 0.037 0.000 0.05 1,107 0.83
LMG unique-variance shares (full M3) — FHA, TdaP [mixed].
term unique_R2_lmg
FHA_IgG1 0.030
FHA_FcgR2a 0.004
FHA_ADCD 0.002
FHA_FcgR3b 0.001

TT mothers (n = 109)

Incremental variance — FHA, TT [mixed].
step R2 dR2 F df p
M1: IgG1 0.075 0.075 NA NA
M2: + FcγR2a/3b 0.091 0.016 0.93 2,105 0.399
M3: + ADCD 0.092 0.001 0.10 1,104 0.753
LMG unique-variance shares (full M3) — FHA, TT [mixed].
term unique_R2_lmg
FHA_IgG1 0.059
FHA_FcgR3b 0.023
FHA_FcgR2a 0.008
FHA_ADCD 0.003

PRN

TdaP mothers (n = 112)

Incremental variance — PRN, TdaP [mixed].
step R2 dR2 F df p
M1: IgG1 0.476 0.476 NA NA
M2: + FcγR2a/3b 0.498 0.022 2.39 2,108 0.0962
M3: + ADCD 0.503 0.004 0.96 1,107 0.3310
LMG unique-variance shares (full M3) — PRN, TdaP [mixed].
term unique_R2_lmg
PRN_IgG1 0.400
PRN_FcgR3b 0.094
PRN_ADCD 0.005
PRN_FcgR2a 0.004

TT mothers (n = 109)

Incremental variance — PRN, TT [mixed].
step R2 dR2 F df p
M1: IgG1 0.151 0.151 NA NA
M2: + FcγR2a/3b 0.168 0.017 1.06 2,105 0.349
M3: + ADCD 0.168 0.000 0.04 1,104 0.840
LMG unique-variance shares (full M3) — PRN, TT [mixed].
term unique_R2_lmg
PRN_IgG1 0.156
PRN_FcgR2a 0.009
PRN_FcgR3b 0.002
PRN_ADCD 0.001

How to read this. Focus on the M3: + ADCD row: a small, non-significant ΔR² means ADCD adds little once IgG1+FcγR are present — the expected footprint of a mediator sitting between FcγR and the outcome. A large significant ΔR² means ADCD carries information the binding measures miss. Read the two arms together: the same ordering (IgG1 large, FcγR incremental, ADCD near-zero) appearing in both arms is strong evidence the mechanism’s shape is arm-invariant.

Step 5 — FcγR Collinearity Diagnostics

In the earlier partial-correlation networks every FcγR3b node dropped to zero — the signature of FcγR2a and FcγR3b being near-collinear, so conditioning on one erases its twin. That is a modelling artifact, not evidence FcγR3b is unimportant. This step recovers the FcγR3b-specific signal you care about by reporting, per antigen and arm: the receptor–receptor correlation, the variance inflation, and — crucially — FcγR3b’s zero-order associations (with the outcome, with IgG1, with ADCD) beside its partial association after adjusting for FcγR2a.

.stage(5, "FcγR collinearity diagnostics")
rows <- list()
for (A in PATHWAY_ANTIGENS) {
  r2a <- paste0(A, "_FcgR2a"); r3b <- paste0(A, "_FcgR3b")
  igg1 <- paste0(A, "_IgG1"); adcd <- paste0(A, "_ADCD")
  if (!all(c(r2a, r3b, igg1) %in% names(path_wide))) next
  for (m in cfg$maternal_arms) {
    need <- keep_present(c(OUTCOME, r2a, r3b, igg1, adcd))
    d <- arm_data(m) %>% dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(need)) %>% tidyr::drop_na()
    if (nrow(d) < 20) next
    .info("step5 %s %s n=%d", A, m, nrow(d))
    rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      antigen = A, arm = m, n = nrow(d),
      cor_2a_3b     = round(cor(d[[r2a]], d[[r3b]]), 3),
      VIF_3b        = round(vif2(d, r3b, r2a, igg1), 2),
      r_3b_OUT      = round(cor(d[[r3b]], d[[OUTCOME]]), 3),
      pr_3b_OUT_2a  = round(pcor_resid(d, r3b, OUTCOME, r2a), 3),
      r_3b_IgG1     = round(cor(d[[r3b]], d[[igg1]]), 3),
      r_3b_ADCD     = if (adcd %in% names(d)) round(cor(d[[r3b]], d[[adcd]]), 3) else NA_real_,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }
}
diag_tbl <- do.call(rbind, rows)
if (is.null(diag_tbl)) {
  cat("*No antigen had a complete FcγR pair; step 5 skipped.*\n\n")
} else {
  print(knitr::kable(diag_tbl, row.names = FALSE,
    caption = "FcγR collinearity and FcγR3b-specific associations (zero-order r vs partial pr | FcγR2a).",
    col.names = c("Antigen", "Arm", "n", "r(2a,3b)", "VIF 3b", "r(3b,OUT)",
                  "pr(3b,OUT|2a)", "r(3b,IgG1)", "r(3b,ADCD)")))
  cat("\n")
}
FcγR collinearity and FcγR3b-specific associations (zero-order r vs partial pr | FcγR2a).
Antigen Arm n r(2a,3b) VIF 3b r(3b,OUT) pr(3b,OUT|2a) r(3b,IgG1) r(3b,ADCD)
PT TdaP 112 0.725 2.74 0.298 0.136 0.760 0.286
PT TT 109 0.325 1.17 0.121 0.036 0.377 0.142
FHA TdaP 112 0.574 1.89 0.158 0.130 0.552 0.165
FHA TT 109 0.496 1.39 -0.038 -0.164 0.240 0.142
PRN TdaP 112 0.750 3.99 0.685 0.469 0.856 0.350
PRN TT 109 0.443 1.26 0.191 0.079 0.423 0.172

How to read this. A high r(2a,3b) (say > 0.8) and VIF 3b well above ~5 confirm the receptors are redundant, explaining why the joint network zeroed FcγR3b. The informative columns are the zero-order associations: a solid r(3b,IgG1) supports IgG1↔︎FcγR3b co-regulation, a solid r(3b,ADCD) supports the FcγR3b→ADCD step, and r(3b,OUT) its link to killing — none of which the partialled network could show. If pr(3b,OUT|2a) collapses toward 0 while r(3b,OUT) is large, the two receptors are interchangeable carriers of the same signal, and FcγR3b-specific claims should rest on the zero-order / single-receptor evidence rather than the joint model. Consistent patterns across arms indicate the redundancy (and the underlying signal) is arm-invariant.

Step 6 — Pathway-Network NCT by Arm

The prior analysis compared only the subclass network across arms and found it invariant. Here the arm comparison (TdaP vs TT, 1000 permutations) is run on the pathway-relevant networks it skipped: the full FcγR + ADCD network, and the focal PRN chain network (IgG1/IgG3 + both FcγRs + ADCD + outcome). Structure invariance (M) tests whether the wiring differs; global-strength invariance (S) tests whether overall connectivity differs.

.stage(6, sprintf("pathway-network NCT by arm (it = %d)", NCT_IT))
nct_pair <- function(nodes, label) {
  nodes <- keep_present(nodes)
  d1 <- arm_data(cfg$maternal_arms[1]) %>% dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(nodes)) %>% tidyr::drop_na()
  d2 <- arm_data(cfg$maternal_arms[2]) %>% dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(nodes)) %>% tidyr::drop_na()
  cat("## ", label, "\n\n", sep = "")
  cat(sprintf("%s n = %d; %s n = %d; %d nodes.\n\n",
              cfg$maternal_arms[1], nrow(d1), cfg$maternal_arms[2], nrow(d2), length(nodes)))
  .info("step6 NCT %-28s n1=%d n2=%d nodes=%d", label, nrow(d1), nrow(d2), length(nodes))
  if (nrow(d1) < NETWORK_MIN || nrow(d2) < NETWORK_MIN) {
    cat(sprintf("*One or both arms n < %d; skipped.*\n\n", NETWORK_MIN)); return(invisible()) }
  res <- tryCatch(NetworkComparisonTest::NCT(d1, d2, it = NCT_IT, test.edges = FALSE,
                    test.centrality = FALSE), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(res)) { cat("*NCT failed.*\n\n"); return(invisible()) }
  cat(sprintf("- Network-structure invariance (M) p = %.3f\n", res$nwinv.pval))
  cat(sprintf("- Global-strength invariance (S) p = %.3f\n\n", res$glstrinv.pval))
}
if (!RUN_NCT || !have_net) {
  cat("*Step 6 skipped (", if (!have_net) "network packages absent" else "run_nct = FALSE", ").*\n\n", sep = "")
} else {
  nct_pair(c(OUTCOME, FCG_ALL, ADCD_ALL), "FcγR + ADCD network — TdaP vs TT")
  nct_pair(c(chain_feats(FOCAL_ANTIGEN), OUTCOME),
           paste0(FOCAL_ANTIGEN, " chain network — TdaP vs TT"))
}

FcγR + ADCD network — TdaP vs TT

TdaP n = 112; TT n = 109; 19 nodes.

|================================================= | 70% | |================================================= | 71% | |================================================== | 71% | |================================================== | 72% | |=================================================== | 72% | |=================================================== | 73% | |=================================================== | 74% | |==================================================== | 74% | |==================================================== | 75% | |===================================================== | 75% | |===================================================== | 76% | |====================================================== | 76% | |====================================================== | 77% | |====================================================== | 78% | |======================================================= | 78% | |======================================================= | 79% | |======================================================== | 79% | |======================================================== | 80% | |======================================================== | 81% | |========================================================= | 81% | |========================================================= | 82% | |========================================================== | 82% | |========================================================== | 83% | |========================================================== | 84% | |=========================================================== | 84% | |=========================================================== | 85% | |============================================================ | 85% | |============================================================ | 86% | |============================================================= | 86% | |============================================================= | 87% | |============================================================= | 88% | |============================================================== | 88% | |================================================================= | 94% | |================================================================== | 94% | |==================================================================== | 97% | |==================================================================== | 98% | |===================================================================== | 98% | |===================================================================== | 99% | |======================================================================| 99% | |======================================================================| 100% - Network-structure invariance (M) p = 0.145 - Global-strength invariance (S) p = 0.733

How to read this. A non-significant M (p > 0.05) means the pathway wiring is statistically indistinguishable between arms — the mechanism is the same shape in TdaP and TT infants, even where the earlier regressions showed different signal strengths. A significant M would flag a genuinely different pathway architecture in one arm. Read together with steps 2–4: non-significant arm interactions and invariant network structure jointly argue the immunological model is supported in both arms, differing in degree rather than in kind.

Step 7 — FcγR3b Unique Role

The joint networks zeroed FcγR3b because it is collinear with FcγR2a. This step separates the two receptors and asks whether FcγR3b carries information independent of FcγR2a — for ADCD (the activation step) and for WT_IgG (the functional endpoint). Two decouplings are used: the residual of FcγR3b on FcγR2a (the part of 3b orthogonal to 2a) and the 2a/3b log-ratio. Run per antigen, per arm, and in each data mode.

.stage(7, "FcγR3b unique role (residual-on-2a and 2a/3b ratio)")
if (!RUN_FCGR3B) {
  cat("*Step 7 skipped (run_fcgr3b = FALSE).*\n\n")
} else {
  .modes <- if (RUN_STD_RERUNS) MODES else "raw"
  for (mode in .modes) {
    cat("## Data mode: `", mode, "`", mode_desc(mode), "\n\n", sep = "")
    wd <- wide_for(mode)
    rows <- list()
    for (A in PATHWAY_ANTIGENS) {
      r2a <- paste0(A, "_FcgR2a"); r3b <- paste0(A, "_FcgR3b"); adcd <- paste0(A, "_ADCD")
      if (!all(c(r2a, r3b) %in% names(wd))) next
      for (m in cfg$maternal_arms) {
        d <- arm_data_m(mode, m)
        # FcγR3b unique contribution to ADCD (activation step)
        uc_adcd <- if (adcd %in% names(wd)) unique_contrib(d, adcd, r3b, r2a) else NULL
        # FcγR3b unique contribution to the whole-cell outcome
        uc_out  <- if (OUTCOME %in% names(wd)) unique_contrib(d, OUTCOME, r3b, r2a) else NULL
        dd <- d[stats::complete.cases(d[, c(r2a, r3b)]), ]
        ratio <- dd[[r3b]] - dd[[r2a]]                       # log(3b/2a)
        r_ratio_out <- if (OUTCOME %in% names(wd))
          suppressWarnings(cor(ratio, dd[[OUTCOME]], use = "complete.obs")) else NA
        rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
          antigen = A, arm = m,
          cor_2a_3b = round(suppressWarnings(cor(dd[[r2a]], dd[[r3b]])), 3),
          "3b|2a→ADCD_dR2" = if (is.null(uc_adcd)) NA else round(uc_adcd$dR2, 3),
          "3b|2a→ADCD_p"   = if (is.null(uc_adcd)) NA else signif(uc_adcd$p, 3),
          "3b|2a→OUT_dR2"  = if (is.null(uc_out))  NA else round(uc_out$dR2, 3),
          "3b|2a→OUT_p"    = if (is.null(uc_out))  NA else signif(uc_out$p, 3),
          "r(3b/2a,OUT)"   = round(r_ratio_out, 3),
          check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
        .info("step7 [%s] %s %s done", mode, A, m)
      }
    }
    tb <- do.call(rbind, rows)
    if (is.null(tb)) { cat("*No complete FcγR pair in this mode.*\n\n"); next }
    print(knitr::kable(tb, row.names = FALSE,
      caption = paste0("FcγR3b unique contribution beyond FcγR2a [", mode,
                       "]: incremental R² and coefficient p of the 3b-residual on ADCD and on the outcome; last column is the 2a/3b ratio's correlation with the outcome."))); cat("\n")
  }
}

Data mode: raw — untransformed log10 (quantity)

FcγR3b unique contribution beyond FcγR2a [raw]: incremental R² and coefficient p of the 3b-residual on ADCD and on the outcome; last column is the 2a/3b ratio’s correlation with the outcome.
antigen arm cor_2a_3b 3b|2a→ADCD_dR2 3b|2a→ADCD_p 3b|2a→OUT_dR2 3b|2a→OUT_p r(3b/2a,OUT)
PT TdaP 0.745 0.048 0.00362 0.023 9.92e-02 0.077
PT TT 0.341 0.001 0.59200 0.001 7.13e-01 -0.115
FHA TdaP 0.601 0.000 0.93500 0.018 1.53e-01 0.124
FHA TT 0.481 0.010 0.17600 0.026 9.04e-02 -0.232
PRN TdaP 0.768 0.014 0.11400 0.147 1.00e-07 0.289
PRN TT 0.336 0.013 0.17600 0.006 4.18e-01 -0.166

Data mode: igg_std — predictors residualized on total IgG (quality); outcome raw

FcγR3b unique contribution beyond FcγR2a [igg_std]: incremental R² and coefficient p of the 3b-residual on ADCD and on the outcome; last column is the 2a/3b ratio’s correlation with the outcome.
antigen arm cor_2a_3b 3b|2a→ADCD_dR2 3b|2a→ADCD_p 3b|2a→OUT_dR2 3b|2a→OUT_p r(3b/2a,OUT)
PT TdaP 0.346 0.230 0.000000 0.019 1.43e-01 0.126
PT TT 0.278 0.040 0.020100 0.004 5.23e-01 -0.056
FHA TdaP 0.392 0.027 0.062200 0.004 4.89e-01 0.090
FHA TT 0.459 0.012 0.194000 0.050 2.02e-02 -0.224
PRN TdaP 0.363 0.092 0.000471 0.158 1.13e-05 0.273
PRN TT 0.339 0.009 0.280000 0.000 9.86e-01 0.038

Data mode: mixed — IgG1/IgG3 raw (quantity exposure); FcγR & ADCD residualized (quality mediators); outcome raw

FcγR3b unique contribution beyond FcγR2a [mixed]: incremental R² and coefficient p of the 3b-residual on ADCD and on the outcome; last column is the 2a/3b ratio’s correlation with the outcome.
antigen arm cor_2a_3b 3b|2a→ADCD_dR2 3b|2a→ADCD_p 3b|2a→OUT_dR2 3b|2a→OUT_p r(3b/2a,OUT)
PT TdaP 0.346 0.230 0.000000 0.019 1.43e-01 0.126
PT TT 0.278 0.040 0.020100 0.004 5.23e-01 -0.056
FHA TdaP 0.392 0.027 0.062200 0.004 4.89e-01 0.090
FHA TT 0.459 0.012 0.194000 0.050 2.02e-02 -0.224
PRN TdaP 0.363 0.092 0.000471 0.158 1.13e-05 0.273
PRN TT 0.339 0.009 0.280000 0.000 9.86e-01 0.038

How to read this. 3b|2a→ADCD columns test the activation claim net of FcγR2a: a significant positive incremental R² means FcγR3b engages complement independently of FcγR2a (supporting the “FcγR3b essential for ADCD” premise); near-zero means the two receptors are interchangeable for ADCD. 3b|2a→OUT does the same for killing. The ratio column asks whether the balance between the two receptors (rather than either level) tracks the outcome. A consistent, significant 3b-unique contribution in one arm but not the other localises the FcγR3b dependence to that arm.

Step 8 — Aggregate Complement vs Whole-Cell Outcome

WT_IgG is a whole-organism readout, whereas ADCD is antigen-specific — a single antigen’s ADCD may under-represent the complement activity relevant to whole-cell killing. This step builds an aggregate complement score (mean antigen-specific ADCD across all available antigens) and tests it against the outcome, alongside the focal PRN-ADCD alone, per arm and per mode. In igg_std mode the ADCD inputs are quantity-removed, so a surviving association is evidence for a complement contribution beyond antibody amount.

.stage(8, "aggregate complement vs whole-cell outcome")
if (!RUN_AGG_ADCD) {
  cat("*Step 8 skipped (run_aggregate_adcd = FALSE).*\n\n")
} else {
  fit_one <- function(d, pred) {
    dd <- d[stats::complete.cases(d[, c(OUTCOME, pred)]), ]
    if (nrow(dd) < 20) return(NULL)
    f <- lm(reformulate(pred, response = OUTCOME), data = dd)
    co <- summary(f)$coefficients[pred, ]
    data.frame(n = nrow(dd), R2 = round(summary(f)$r.squared, 3),
               beta = round(unname(co[1]), 3), p = signif(unname(co[4]), 3),
               stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }
  .modes <- if (RUN_STD_RERUNS) MODES else "raw"
  for (mode in .modes) {
    cat("## Data mode: `", mode, "`", mode_desc(mode), "\n\n", sep = "")
    wd <- wide_for(mode)
    adcd_cols <- intersect(paste0(ALL_ANTIGENS, "_ADCD"), names(wd))
    if (!length(adcd_cols) || !(OUTCOME %in% names(wd))) { cat("*No ADCD columns / outcome in this mode.*\n\n"); next }
    focal_adcd <- paste0(FOCAL_ANTIGEN, "_ADCD")
    rows <- list()
    for (m in cfg$maternal_arms) {
      d <- arm_data_m(mode, m)
      d$ADCD_aggregate <- rowMeans(d[, adcd_cols, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
      agg <- fit_one(d, "ADCD_aggregate")
      foc <- if (focal_adcd %in% names(d)) fit_one(d, focal_adcd) else NULL
      if (!is.null(agg)) rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- cbind(arm = m, predictor = paste0("aggregate ADCD (", length(adcd_cols), " antigens)"), agg)
      if (!is.null(foc)) rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- cbind(arm = m, predictor = paste0(FOCAL_ANTIGEN, "_ADCD (focal)"), foc)
      .info("step8 [%s] %s aggregate+focal done", mode, m)
    }
    tb <- do.call(rbind, rows)
    if (is.null(tb)) { cat("*Too few complete cases.*\n\n"); next }
    print(knitr::kable(tb, row.names = FALSE,
      caption = paste0("Univariate association of complement scores with ", cfg$outcome_label,
                       " [", mode, "]: aggregate (mean ADCD across antigens) vs focal ", FOCAL_ANTIGEN, "-ADCD."))); cat("\n")
  }
}

Data mode: raw — untransformed log10 (quantity)

Univariate association of complement scores with WT_IgG [raw]: aggregate (mean ADCD across antigens) vs focal PRN-ADCD.
arm predictor n R2 beta p
TdaP aggregate ADCD (6 antigens) 112 0.054 0.868 1.38e-02
TdaP PRN_ADCD (focal) 112 0.133 0.896 7.48e-05
TT aggregate ADCD (6 antigens) 109 0.040 0.307 3.66e-02
TT PRN_ADCD (focal) 109 0.016 0.131 1.90e-01

Data mode: igg_std — predictors residualized on total IgG (quality); outcome raw

Univariate association of complement scores with WT_IgG [igg_std]: aggregate (mean ADCD across antigens) vs focal PRN-ADCD.
arm predictor n R2 beta p
TdaP aggregate ADCD (6 antigens) 112 0.000 0.024 0.957
TdaP PRN_ADCD (focal) 112 0.001 0.115 0.705
TT aggregate ADCD (6 antigens) 109 0.000 0.042 0.823
TT PRN_ADCD (focal) 109 0.001 -0.043 0.697

Data mode: mixed — IgG1/IgG3 raw (quantity exposure); FcγR & ADCD residualized (quality mediators); outcome raw

Univariate association of complement scores with WT_IgG [mixed]: aggregate (mean ADCD across antigens) vs focal PRN-ADCD.
arm predictor n R2 beta p
TdaP aggregate ADCD (6 antigens) 112 0.000 0.024 0.957
TdaP PRN_ADCD (focal) 112 0.001 0.115 0.705
TT aggregate ADCD (6 antigens) 109 0.000 0.042 0.823
TT PRN_ADCD (focal) 109 0.001 -0.043 0.697

How to read this. If the aggregate score predicts the whole-cell outcome more strongly than focal PRN-ADCD, whole-cell killing draws on complement activity spread across antigens, and a single-antigen ADCD node understated the pathway in earlier steps. In igg_std mode a significant aggregate association is the cleanest evidence that complement deposition contributes to the outcome independently of antibody quantity; if it vanishes under standardization, the raw ADCD–outcome association was quantity-driven. Compare arms for whether the complement contribution is present in both TdaP and TT.

Step 9 — Arm × FcγR Interaction Tests

Steps 4 and 7 showed the FcγR(→SBA) coupling descriptively stronger in TdaP than TT. This step turns that into a formal test: whether the FcγR→outcome mapping differs by maternal arm, rather than differing only in the amount pushed through a shared mapping. Two tests per antigen and mode: a focal FcγR3b × arm interaction on WT_IgG, and a likelihood-ratio test of the whole FcγR block × arm (does adding arm-interaction terms to FcγR2a + FcγR3b improve fit). Predictors are standardized within the pooled sample; reference arm is TdaP.

.stage(9, "arm × FcγR interaction tests on the outcome")
if (!RUN_ARM_TESTS) {
  cat("*Step 9 skipped (run_arm_tests = FALSE).*\n\n")
} else {
  .modes <- if (RUN_STD_RERUNS) MODES else "raw"
  for (mode in .modes) {
    cat("## Data mode: `", mode, "`", mode_desc(mode), "\n\n", sep = "")
    wd <- wide_for(mode)
    if (!(OUTCOME %in% names(wd)) || !("arm_name" %in% names(wd))) { cat("*Outcome/arm missing.*\n\n"); next }
    rows <- list()
    for (A in PATHWAY_ANTIGENS) {
      r2a <- paste0(A, "_FcgR2a"); r3b <- paste0(A, "_FcgR3b")
      if (!all(c(r2a, r3b) %in% names(wd))) next
      d <- wd %>% dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(c(OUTCOME, r2a, r3b, "arm_name"))) %>% tidyr::drop_na()
      if (nrow(d) < 40) next
      d[[r2a]] <- as.numeric(scale(d[[r2a]])); d[[r3b]] <- as.numeric(scale(d[[r3b]]))
      d$arm <- factor(d$arm_name, levels = cfg$maternal_arms)
      # focal: FcγR3b × arm
      fA <- lm(stats::as.formula(paste0(OUTCOME, " ~ ", r3b, " * arm")), data = d)
      ci <- summary(fA)$coefficients
      irow <- grep(paste0("^", r3b, ":arm"), rownames(ci))
      # FcγR block × arm: LRT (F-test) of interaction terms
      f_add <- lm(stats::as.formula(paste0(OUTCOME, " ~ (", r2a, " + ", r3b, ") + arm")), data = d)
      f_int <- lm(stats::as.formula(paste0(OUTCOME, " ~ (", r2a, " + ", r3b, ") * arm")), data = d)
      av <- anova(f_add, f_int)
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        antigen = A, n = nrow(d),
        "FcγR3b×arm β" = round(if (length(irow)) ci[irow[1], 1] else NA, 3),
        "FcγR3b×arm p" = signif(if (length(irow)) ci[irow[1], 4] else NA, 3),
        "block×arm F"  = round(av$F[2], 2),
        "block×arm df" = paste0(av$Df[2], ",", av$Res.Df[2]),
        "block×arm p"  = signif(av$`Pr(>F)`[2], 3),
        check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
      .info("step9 [%s] %s interaction done (n=%d)", mode, A, nrow(d))
    }
    tb <- do.call(rbind, rows)
    if (is.null(tb)) { cat("*No antigen had a complete FcγR pair.*\n\n"); next }
    print(knitr::kable(tb, row.names = FALSE,
      caption = paste0("Arm-modification of the FcγR\u2192", cfg$outcome_label,
                       " mapping [", mode, "]: focal FcγR3b\u00d7arm coefficient, and F-test of the whole FcγR-block\u00d7arm interaction (reference arm ", cfg$maternal_arms[1], ")."))); cat("\n")
  }
}

Data mode: raw — untransformed log10 (quantity)

Arm-modification of the FcγR→WT_IgG mapping [raw]: focal FcγR3b×arm coefficient, and F-test of the whole FcγR-block×arm interaction (reference arm TdaP).
antigen n FcγR3b×arm β FcγR3b×arm p block×arm F block×arm df block×arm p
PT 223 -0.134 0.206 0.60 2,217 0.550
FHA 223 -0.173 0.168 2.00 2,217 0.137
PRN 223 -0.179 0.213 1.13 2,217 0.324

Data mode: igg_std — predictors residualized on total IgG (quality); outcome raw

Arm-modification of the FcγR→WT_IgG mapping [igg_std]: focal FcγR3b×arm coefficient, and F-test of the whole FcγR-block×arm interaction (reference arm TdaP).
antigen n FcγR3b×arm β FcγR3b×arm p block×arm F block×arm df block×arm p
PT 223 -0.107 0.14500 1.22 2,217 0.29700
FHA 223 -0.077 0.29700 1.97 2,217 0.14200
PRN 223 -0.250 0.00141 4.95 2,217 0.00789

Data mode: mixed — IgG1/IgG3 raw (quantity exposure); FcγR & ADCD residualized (quality mediators); outcome raw

Arm-modification of the FcγR→WT_IgG mapping [mixed]: focal FcγR3b×arm coefficient, and F-test of the whole FcγR-block×arm interaction (reference arm TdaP).
antigen n FcγR3b×arm β FcγR3b×arm p block×arm F block×arm df block×arm p
PT 223 -0.107 0.14500 1.22 2,217 0.29700
FHA 223 -0.077 0.29700 1.97 2,217 0.14200
PRN 223 -0.250 0.00141 4.95 2,217 0.00789

How to read this. A significant FcγR3b × arm term means the per-unit FcγR3b→outcome slope genuinely differs between TdaP and TT — evidence the pathway is wired differently, not merely driven harder in one arm. A significant block × arm LRT says the same for the FcγR layer as a whole. Non-significant interactions with different per-arm strengths (from steps 4/7) would instead point to different forcing through a shared mapping. Read alongside the mixed mode, where the FcγR/ADCD mediators are quality (quantity-removed): a surviving arm interaction there localises the divergence to per-antibody engagement rather than to antibody amount.

Step 10 — FcγR3b vs ADCD for the Outcome

The earlier steps produced a puzzle: FcγR3b predicts WT_IgG (in TdaP, quantity-removed), yet ADCD does not — even though WT_IgG is complement-mediated killing and ADCD is complement deposition. This step adjudicates directly: within one model, does FcγR3b survive after controlling for ADCD, and does ADCD retain anything after FcγR3b? If FcγR3b’s association is independent of the measured ADCD, whole-cell killing depends on FcγR3b engagement through a route this ADCD assay does not capture. Fit per antigen, per arm, per mode; incremental R² isolates each term’s unique contribution.

.stage(10, "FcγR3b vs ADCD for the outcome")
if (!RUN_FCGR3B_ADCD) {
  cat("*Step 10 skipped (run_fcgr3b_adcd = FALSE).*\n\n")
} else {
  dR2 <- function(d, y, add, base) {
    b <- lm(reformulate(base, response = y), data = d)
    f <- lm(reformulate(c(base, add), response = y), data = d)
    summary(f)$r.squared - summary(b)$r.squared
  }
  .modes <- if (RUN_STD_RERUNS) MODES else "raw"
  for (mode in .modes) {
    cat("## Data mode: `", mode, "`", mode_desc(mode), "\n\n", sep = "")
    wd <- wide_for(mode)
    rows <- list()
    for (A in PATHWAY_ANTIGENS) {
      r3b <- paste0(A, "_FcgR3b"); adcd <- paste0(A, "_ADCD")
      if (!all(c(r3b, adcd, OUTCOME) %in% names(wd))) next
      for (m in cfg$maternal_arms) {
        d <- arm_data_m(mode, m) %>% dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(c(OUTCOME, r3b, adcd))) %>% tidyr::drop_na()
        if (nrow(d) < 20) next
        f <- lm(reformulate(c(r3b, adcd), response = OUTCOME), data = d)
        co <- summary(f)$coefficients
        rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
          antigen = A, arm = m, n = nrow(d), R2_joint = round(summary(f)$r.squared, 3),
          "β FcγR3b" = round(co[r3b, 1], 3), "p FcγR3b" = signif(co[r3b, 4], 3),
          "β ADCD"   = round(co[adcd, 1], 3), "p ADCD"   = signif(co[adcd, 4], 3),
          "ΔR² 3b|ADCD" = round(dR2(d, OUTCOME, r3b, adcd), 3),
          "ΔR² ADCD|3b" = round(dR2(d, OUTCOME, adcd, r3b), 3),
          check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
        .info("step10 [%s] %s %s done (n=%d)", mode, A, m, nrow(d))
      }
    }
    tb <- do.call(rbind, rows)
    if (is.null(tb)) { cat("*No antigen had FcγR3b + ADCD + outcome complete.*\n\n"); next }
    print(knitr::kable(tb, row.names = FALSE,
      caption = paste0("FcγR3b vs ADCD as joint predictors of ", cfg$outcome_label,
                       " [", mode, "]: coefficients, and each term's incremental R² beyond the other."))); cat("\n")
  }
}

Data mode: raw — untransformed log10 (quantity)

FcγR3b vs ADCD as joint predictors of WT_IgG [raw]: coefficients, and each term’s incremental R² beyond the other.
antigen arm n R2_joint β FcγR3b p FcγR3b β ADCD p ADCD ΔR² 3b|ADCD ΔR² ADCD|3b
PT TdaP 112 0.095 0.363 0.0047 0.239 0.3900 0.069 0.006
PT TT 109 0.055 0.113 0.3370 0.169 0.0364 0.008 0.040
FHA TdaP 112 0.032 0.187 0.1340 0.267 0.3800 0.020 0.007
FHA TT 109 0.047 -0.070 0.4720 0.202 0.0258 0.005 0.046
PRN TdaP 112 0.487 0.489 0.0000 0.351 0.0533 0.354 0.018
PRN TT 109 0.046 0.275 0.0730 0.100 0.3190 0.030 0.009

Data mode: igg_std — predictors residualized on total IgG (quality); outcome raw

FcγR3b vs ADCD as joint predictors of WT_IgG [igg_std]: coefficients, and each term’s incremental R² beyond the other.
antigen arm n R2_joint β FcγR3b p FcγR3b β ADCD p ADCD ΔR² 3b|ADCD ΔR² ADCD|3b
PT TdaP 112 0.024 0.157 4.47e-01 -0.430 0.278 0.005 0.011
PT TT 109 0.020 -0.045 6.78e-01 0.145 0.191 0.002 0.016
FHA TdaP 112 0.004 0.031 8.41e-01 -0.188 0.570 0.000 0.003
FHA TT 109 0.030 -0.131 1.10e-01 0.108 0.414 0.024 0.006
PRN TdaP 112 0.177 0.637 4.50e-06 0.413 0.148 0.176 0.016
PRN TT 109 0.003 -0.046 7.21e-01 -0.036 0.746 0.001 0.001

Data mode: mixed — IgG1/IgG3 raw (quantity exposure); FcγR & ADCD residualized (quality mediators); outcome raw

FcγR3b vs ADCD as joint predictors of WT_IgG [mixed]: coefficients, and each term’s incremental R² beyond the other.
antigen arm n R2_joint β FcγR3b p FcγR3b β ADCD p ADCD ΔR² 3b|ADCD ΔR² ADCD|3b
PT TdaP 112 0.024 0.157 4.47e-01 -0.430 0.278 0.005 0.011
PT TT 109 0.020 -0.045 6.78e-01 0.145 0.191 0.002 0.016
FHA TdaP 112 0.004 0.031 8.41e-01 -0.188 0.570 0.000 0.003
FHA TT 109 0.030 -0.131 1.10e-01 0.108 0.414 0.024 0.006
PRN TdaP 112 0.177 0.637 4.50e-06 0.413 0.148 0.176 0.016
PRN TT 109 0.003 -0.046 7.21e-01 -0.036 0.746 0.001 0.001

How to read this. ΔR² 3b|ADCD is the variance FcγR3b adds beyond ADCD; ΔR² ADCD|3b the reverse. A significant FcγR3b coefficient with a clearly larger ΔR² 3b|ADCD than ΔR² ADCD|3b, alongside a non-significant ADCD term, is direct evidence that FcγR3b’s link to killing does not run through the measured ADCD — implicating either a complement route the bead-based ADCD assay misses or a non-ADCD FcγR3b function. If instead ADCD dominates, the pathway’s complement step is captured as intended. Compare arms and modes: the mixed mode (quality mediators, quantity exposure kept upstream) is the fairest read of the per-antibody effector step.

Step 11 — Effector Activation by FcγR

The chain analysis treated ADCD as the effector node. Here we ask the same activation question of all three antibody-dependent effector functions — complement deposition (ADCD), cellular phagocytosis (ADCP), and neutrophil phagocytosis (ADNP): is each engaged by FcγR2a/FcγR3b? For every effector present for a given antigen we regress the effector on the two receptors, isolate the FcγR3b-specific contribution (residual on FcγR2a), and test whether the FcγR→effector mapping differs by maternal arm. ADCP and ADNP are available for only a subset of antigens; absent effector×antigen cells are skipped.

.stage(11, "effector activation by FcγR (ADCD / ADCP / ADNP)")
if (!RUN_EFF_ACT) {
  cat("*Step 11 skipped (run_effector_activation = FALSE).*\n\n")
} else {
  .modes <- if (RUN_STD_RERUNS) MODES else "raw"
  for (mode in .modes) {
    cat("## Data mode: `", mode, "`", mode_desc(mode), "\n\n", sep = "")
    wd <- wide_for(mode)
    rows <- list(); irows <- list()
    for (fn in EFFECTORS) {
      for (A in PATHWAY_ANTIGENS) {
        eff <- paste0(A, "_", fn); r2a <- paste0(A, "_FcgR2a"); r3b <- paste0(A, "_FcgR3b")
        if (!all(c(eff, r2a, r3b) %in% names(wd))) next
        for (m in cfg$maternal_arms) {
          d <- arm_data_m(mode, m) %>% dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(c(eff, r2a, r3b))) %>% tidyr::drop_na()
          if (nrow(d) < NETWORK_MIN) next
          f <- lm(reformulate(c(r2a, r3b), response = eff), data = d)
          co <- summary(f)$coefficients
          d$.fres <- resid(lm(reformulate(r2a, response = r3b), data = d))
          base <- lm(reformulate(r2a, response = eff), data = d)
          full <- lm(reformulate(c(r2a, ".fres"), response = eff), data = d)
          uc <- summary(full)$coefficients[".fres", ]
          rows[[length(rows)+1]] <- data.frame(
            effector = fn, antigen = A, arm = m, n = nrow(d),
            R2 = round(summary(f)$r.squared, 3),
            "β FcγR2a" = round(co[r2a,1],3), "p FcγR2a" = signif(co[r2a,4],3),
            "β FcγR3b" = round(co[r3b,1],3), "p FcγR3b" = signif(co[r3b,4],3),
            "3b|2a ΔR²" = round(summary(full)$r.squared - summary(base)$r.squared, 3),
            "3b|2a p" = signif(uc[4],3), check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
        }
        # arm interaction on the FcγR->effector mapping (pooled, standardized predictors)
        di <- wd %>% dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(c(eff, r2a, r3b, "arm_name"))) %>% tidyr::drop_na()
        if (nrow(di) >= 40) {
          di[[r2a]] <- as.numeric(scale(di[[r2a]])); di[[r3b]] <- as.numeric(scale(di[[r3b]]))
          di$arm <- factor(di$arm_name, levels = cfg$maternal_arms)
          f_add <- lm(reformulate(c(r2a, r3b, "arm"), response = eff), data = di)
          f_int <- lm(stats::as.formula(paste0(eff, " ~ (", r2a, " + ", r3b, ") * arm")), data = di)
          av <- anova(f_add, f_int)
          irows[[length(irows)+1]] <- data.frame(
            effector = fn, antigen = A, n = nrow(di),
            "block×arm F" = round(av$F[2],2), "block×arm p" = signif(av$`Pr(>F)`[2],3),
            check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
        }
        .info("step11 [%s] %s %s activation done", mode, fn, A)
      }
    }
    tb <- do.call(rbind, rows)
    if (is.null(tb)) { cat("*No effector×antigen present in this mode.*\n\n"); next }
    print(knitr::kable(tb, row.names = FALSE,
      caption = paste0("Effector activation by FcγR [", mode,
      "]: effector regressed on FcγR2a + FcγR3b, with the FcγR3b-specific (residual-on-2a) contribution."))); cat("\n")
    ib <- do.call(rbind, irows)
    if (!is.null(ib)) print(knitr::kable(ib, row.names = FALSE,
      caption = paste0("Arm-modification of the FcγR→effector mapping [", mode,
      "]: F-test of the FcγR-block × arm interaction (reference ", cfg$maternal_arms[1], ")."))); cat("\n")
  }
}

Data mode: raw — untransformed log10 (quantity)

Arm-modification of the FcγR→effector mapping [igg_std]: F-test of the FcγR-block × arm interaction (reference TdaP).
effector antigen n block×arm F block×arm p
ADCD PT 262 0.28 7.56e-01
ADCD FHA 262 1.62 2.00e-01
ADCD PRN 262 3.30 3.84e-02
ADCP PT 253 10.20 5.54e-05
ADCP PRN 253 1.45 2.38e-01
ADNP PT 253 0.23 7.96e-01
ADNP PRN 253 11.10 2.41e-05

Data mode: mixed — IgG1/IgG3 raw (quantity exposure); FcγR & ADCD residualized (quality mediators); outcome raw

Effector activation by FcγR [mixed]: effector regressed on FcγR2a + FcγR3b, with the FcγR3b-specific (residual-on-2a) contribution.
effector antigen arm n R2 β FcγR2a p FcγR2a β FcγR3b p FcγR3b 3b|2a ΔR² 3b|2a p
ADCD PT TdaP 128 0.260 0.215 3.28e-05 -0.281 0.00e+00 0.230 0.00e+00
ADCD PT TT 134 0.064 0.235 1.59e-02 -0.216 2.01e-02 0.040 2.01e-02
ADCD FHA TdaP 128 0.031 0.085 1.79e-01 -0.089 6.22e-02 0.027 6.22e-02
ADCD FHA TT 134 0.069 0.240 2.36e-03 -0.076 1.94e-01 0.012 1.94e-01
ADCD PRN TdaP 128 0.107 0.122 8.80e-03 -0.154 4.71e-04 0.092 4.71e-04
ADCD PRN TT 134 0.031 0.093 2.06e-01 0.120 2.80e-01 0.009 2.80e-01
ADCP PT TdaP 126 0.124 -0.068 2.99e-01 0.243 6.11e-05 0.123 6.11e-05
ADCP PT TT 127 0.292 0.288 0.00e+00 0.088 6.17e-02 0.020 6.17e-02
ADCP PRN TdaP 126 0.065 -0.040 2.54e-01 0.097 4.15e-03 0.065 4.15e-03
ADCP PRN TT 127 0.016 0.031 2.96e-01 0.023 6.35e-01 0.002 6.35e-01
ADNP PT TdaP 126 0.025 -0.013 8.45e-01 0.100 8.61e-02 0.024 8.61e-02
ADNP PT TT 127 0.008 0.005 9.26e-01 0.048 3.42e-01 0.007 3.42e-01
ADNP PRN TdaP 126 0.312 -0.185 1.84e-04 0.331 0.00e+00 0.301 0.00e+00
ADNP PRN TT 127 0.215 0.106 7.87e-03 0.238 2.54e-04 0.090 2.54e-04
Arm-modification of the FcγR→effector mapping [mixed]: F-test of the FcγR-block × arm interaction (reference TdaP).
effector antigen n block×arm F block×arm p
ADCD PT 262 0.28 7.56e-01
ADCD FHA 262 1.62 2.00e-01
ADCD PRN 262 3.30 3.84e-02
ADCP PT 253 10.20 5.54e-05
ADCP PRN 253 1.45 2.38e-01
ADNP PT 253 0.23 7.96e-01
ADNP PRN 253 11.10 2.41e-05

How to read this. A significant positive FcγR coefficient means that effector is antibody-Fc-driven in that arm/antigen; comparing the three functions shows whether FcγR engagement feeds complement (ADCD), monocyte phagocytosis (ADCP) and neutrophil phagocytosis (ADNP) equally or selectively. The 3b|2a columns isolate the FcγR3b-specific drive (the receptor implicated for SBA in the ADCD chain). A significant block×arm interaction indicates the FcγR→effector coupling itself differs between TdaP and TT infants, not just its inputs.

Step 12 — Each Effector on the Path to the Outcome

For ADCD we found no independent contribution to WT_IgG once FcγR3b was accounted for. Here we apply the same test to ADCP and ADNP: for each effector, antigen, arm and mode, does it add variance in WT_IgG beyond antibody quantity (IgG1) and beyond IgG1 + FcγR, and does it survive jointly with FcγR3b? An effector that adds nothing beyond FcγR, or that drops out against FcγR3b, is not independently on the path.

.stage(12, "each effector on the path to the outcome (ADCD / ADCP / ADNP)")
if (!RUN_EFF_SBA) {
  cat("*Step 12 skipped (run_effector_to_sba = FALSE).*\n\n")
} else {
  dR2 <- function(d, y, add, base) {
    b <- lm(reformulate(base, response = y), data = d)
    f <- lm(reformulate(c(base, add), response = y), data = d)
    ff <- summary(f)$fstatistic
    p <- tryCatch(anova(b, f)$`Pr(>F)`[2], error = function(e) NA)
    list(dR2 = summary(f)$r.squared - summary(b)$r.squared, p = p)
  }
  .modes <- if (RUN_STD_RERUNS) MODES else "raw"
  for (mode in .modes) {
    cat("## Data mode: `", mode, "`", mode_desc(mode), "\n\n", sep = "")
    wd <- wide_for(mode)
    rows <- list()
    for (fn in EFFECTORS) {
      for (A in PATHWAY_ANTIGENS) {
        eff <- paste0(A, "_", fn); igg1 <- paste0(A, "_IgG1")
        r2a <- paste0(A, "_FcgR2a"); r3b <- paste0(A, "_FcgR3b")
        if (!all(c(eff, igg1, r3b, OUTCOME) %in% names(wd))) next
        fcg <- intersect(c(r2a, r3b), names(wd))
        for (m in cfg$maternal_arms) {
          d <- arm_data_m(mode, m) %>%
            dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(unique(c(OUTCOME, eff, igg1, fcg)))) %>% tidyr::drop_na()
          if (nrow(d) < NETWORK_MIN) next
          inc_q  <- dR2(d, OUTCOME, eff, igg1)              # beyond IgG1 (quantity)
          inc_f  <- dR2(d, OUTCOME, eff, c(igg1, fcg))      # beyond IgG1 + FcγR
          fj <- lm(reformulate(c(r3b, eff), response = OUTCOME), data = d)  # joint with FcγR3b
          cj <- summary(fj)$coefficients
          rows[[length(rows)+1]] <- data.frame(
            effector = fn, antigen = A, arm = m, n = nrow(d),
            "ΔR² eff|IgG1" = round(inc_q$dR2, 3), "p(|IgG1)" = signif(inc_q$p, 3),
            "ΔR² eff|IgG1+FcγR" = round(inc_f$dR2, 3), "p(|IgG1+FcγR)" = signif(inc_f$p, 3),
            "β eff (joint w/3b)" = round(cj[eff,1], 3), "p eff" = signif(cj[eff,4], 3),
            "β FcγR3b" = round(cj[r3b,1], 3), "p 3b" = signif(cj[r3b,4], 3),
            check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
          .info("step12 [%s] %s %s %s done", mode, fn, A, m)
        }
      }
    }
    tb <- do.call(rbind, rows)
    if (is.null(tb)) { cat("*No complete effector chain in this mode.*\n\n"); next }
    print(knitr::kable(tb, row.names = FALSE,
      caption = paste0("Each effector on the path to ", cfg$outcome_label, " [", mode,
      "]: incremental R² beyond IgG1 and beyond IgG1+FcγR, and coefficients in a joint model with FcγR3b."))); cat("\n")
  }
}

Data mode: raw — untransformed log10 (quantity)

Each effector on the path to WT_IgG [raw]: incremental R² beyond IgG1 and beyond IgG1+FcγR, and coefficients in a joint model with FcγR3b.
effector antigen arm n ΔR² eff|IgG1 p(|IgG1) ΔR² eff|IgG1+FcγR p(|IgG1+FcγR) β eff (joint w/3b) p eff β FcγR3b p 3b

ADCD PT TdaP 112 0.023 0.11500 0.011 0.27000 -0.430 0.27800 0.157 4.47e-01
ADCD PT TT 109 0.018 0.17000 0.013 0.23600 0.145 0.19100 -0.045 6.78e-01
ADCD FHA TdaP 112 0.004 0.51700 0.002 0.62100 -0.188 0.57000 0.031 8.41e-01
ADCD FHA TT 109 0.000 0.84200 0.002 0.64400 0.108 0.41400 -0.131 1.10e-01

effector antigen arm n ΔR² eff|IgG1 p(|IgG1) ΔR² eff|IgG1+FcγR p(|IgG1+FcγR) β eff (joint w/3b) p eff β FcγR3b p 3b
ADCD PT TdaP 112 0.017 0.1420 0.020 0.1180 -0.430 0.27800 0.157 4.47e-01
ADCD PT TT 109 0.000 0.9170 0.000 0.9210 0.145 0.19100 -0.045 6.78e-01
ADCD FHA TdaP 112 0.001 0.7740 0.000 0.8300 -0.188 0.57000 0.031 8.41e-01
ADCD FHA TT 109 0.000 0.9990 0.001 0.7530 0.108 0.41400 -0.131 1.10e-01
ADCD PRN TdaP 112 0.000 0.7480 0.004 0.3310 0.413 0.14800 0.637 4.50e-06
ADCD PRN TT 109 0.005 0.4530 0.000 0.8400 -0.036 0.74600 -0.046 7.21e-01
ADCP PT TdaP 110 0.000 0.9380 0.000 0.9820 0.096 0.78300 0.210 3.13e-01
ADCP PT TT 105 0.017 0.1670 0.022 0.1190 -0.320 0.21200 -0.018 8.73e-01
ADCP PRN TdaP 110 0.002 0.4710 0.001 0.5970 0.744 0.06420 0.543 9.19e-05
ADCP PRN TT 105 0.030 0.0526 0.029 0.0575 0.786 0.00607 -0.013 9.18e-01
ADNP PT TdaP 110 0.027 0.0652 0.027 0.0647 0.710 0.03380 0.188 3.33e-01
ADNP PT TT 105 0.005 0.4680 0.004 0.5050 0.240 0.27400 -0.080 4.62e-01
ADNP PRN TdaP 110 0.002 0.5150 0.000 0.9820 0.472 0.07960 0.477 1.57e-03
ADNP PRN TT 105 0.013 0.2090 0.020 0.1180 -0.234 0.27500 0.090 5.41e-01

How to read this. ΔR² eff|IgG1 asks whether the effector tracks WT_IgG at all once quantity is set; ΔR² eff|IgG1+FcγR is the stricter test of an independent effector contribution beyond receptor engagement. In the joint column, an effector that keeps a significant coefficient while FcγR3b weakens would be genuinely on the path; the ADCD pattern (effector drops, FcγR3b survives) means the function is a correlate of FcγR3b rather than an independent step. Compare ADCP and ADNP against ADCD, and TdaP against TT, in the quantity-removed (igg_std/mixed) modes.

Step 13 — Antigen-Specificity and Multi-Effector Model

Two final questions: is any effector→WT_IgG association antigen-specific or broad (focal PRN vs an aggregate mean across the antigens carrying that effector), and when the three functions compete in one model, which — if any — is independently on the path? Reported per arm and mode; the aggregate uses each effector’s own available-antigen set.

.stage(13, "antigen-specificity and multi-effector model")
if (!RUN_EFF_AGG) {
  cat("*Step 13 skipped (run_effector_aggregate = FALSE).*\n\n")
} else {
  fit1 <- function(d, pred) {
    dd <- d[stats::complete.cases(d[, c(OUTCOME, pred)]), , drop = FALSE]
    if (nrow(dd) < NETWORK_MIN) return(NULL)
    f <- lm(reformulate(pred, response = OUTCOME), data = dd); co <- summary(f)$coefficients[pred, ]
    data.frame(n = nrow(dd), R2 = round(summary(f)$r.squared, 3),
               beta = round(unname(co[1]), 3), p = signif(unname(co[4]), 3))
  }
  .modes <- if (RUN_STD_RERUNS) MODES else "raw"
  for (mode in .modes) {
    cat("## Data mode: `", mode, "`", mode_desc(mode), "\n\n", sep = "")
    wd <- wide_for(mode)
    if (!(OUTCOME %in% names(wd))) { cat("*Outcome missing.*\n\n"); next }
    # build focal + aggregate columns per effector
    agg_names <- c()
    for (fn in EFFECTORS) {
      cols <- intersect(eff_all(fn), names(wd))
      if (length(cols)) { wd[[paste0("agg_", fn)]] <- rowMeans(wd[, cols, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
                          agg_names <- c(agg_names, paste0("agg_", fn)) }
    }
    strat <- setNames(lapply(cfg$maternal_arms, function(m) dplyr::filter(wd, arm_name == m)), cfg$maternal_arms)

    # (a) focal vs aggregate, per effector
    rows <- list()
    for (fn in EFFECTORS) {
      foc <- paste0(FOCAL_ANTIGEN, "_", fn); agg <- paste0("agg_", fn)
      for (m in cfg$maternal_arms) {
        d <- strat[[m]]
        rf <- if (foc %in% names(d)) fit1(d, foc) else NULL
        ra <- if (agg %in% names(d)) fit1(d, agg) else NULL
        if (!is.null(rf)) rows[[length(rows)+1]] <- cbind(effector = fn, arm = m, scope = paste0("focal ", FOCAL_ANTIGEN), rf)
        if (!is.null(ra)) rows[[length(rows)+1]] <- cbind(effector = fn, arm = m,
          scope = paste0("aggregate (", length(intersect(eff_all(fn), names(wd))), " antigens)"), ra)
      }
    }
    tb <- do.call(rbind, rows)
    if (!is.null(tb)) print(knitr::kable(tb, row.names = FALSE,
      caption = paste0("Antigen-specificity of effector\u2192", cfg$outcome_label, " [", mode,
      "]: focal ", FOCAL_ANTIGEN, " vs aggregate score, univariate."))); cat("\n")

    # (b) joint focal three-effector model; (c) joint aggregate model
    for (basis in c("focal", "aggregate")) {
      preds <- if (basis == "focal") paste0(FOCAL_ANTIGEN, "_", EFFECTORS) else paste0("agg_", EFFECTORS)
      jrows <- list()
      for (m in cfg$maternal_arms) {
        d <- strat[[m]]; pp <- intersect(preds, names(d))
        dd <- d[stats::complete.cases(d[, c(OUTCOME, pp)]), , drop = FALSE]
        if (length(pp) < 2 || nrow(dd) < NETWORK_MIN) next
        f <- lm(reformulate(pp, response = OUTCOME), data = dd); co <- summary(f)$coefficients
        for (p in pp) jrows[[length(jrows)+1]] <- data.frame(
          arm = m, n = nrow(dd), R2_model = round(summary(f)$r.squared, 3),
          effector = sub(paste0("^", FOCAL_ANTIGEN, "_|^agg_"), "", p),
          beta = round(co[p,1],3), p = signif(co[p,4],3), check.names = FALSE)
      }
      jb <- do.call(rbind, jrows)
      if (!is.null(jb)) print(knitr::kable(jb, row.names = FALSE,
        caption = paste0("Joint ", basis, " ADCD+ADCP+ADNP model for ", cfg$outcome_label,
        " [", mode, "]: which effector is independently associated."))); cat("\n")
    }
    .info("step13 [%s] done", mode)
  }
}

Data mode: raw — untransformed log10 (quantity)

Antigen-specificity of effector→WT_IgG [raw]: focal PRN vs aggregate score, univariate.

arm n R2_model effector beta p
TdaP 110 0.070 ADCD 0.197 0.6640

TdaP 110 0.070 ADNP 1.371 0.0202
TT 105 0.013 ADCD 0.173 0.3730
TT 105 0.013 ADCP -0.146 0.7660
TT 105 0.013 ADNP 0.224 0.5030

How to read this. If the aggregate score outperforms focal PRN, the effector’s link to WT_IgG is broad across antigens rather than pertactin-specific. In the joint models, a single effector retaining a significant coefficient identifies the function that is independently on the path; if all three collapse (as ADCD did against FcγR3b), the effector functions are collinear correlates of receptor engagement rather than separable steps. The quantity-removed modes are decisive.

Participant Accounting

message(sprintf("[%s | +%5.1fs] pathway assessment DONE", .hms(), .el()))
nt_render(nt, caption = "Participant accounting across all pathway steps.")
Participant accounting across all pathway steps.
Section Stratum Model N entered N used N dropped Dropped due to (NA driver)
Step1 net: PT PT @ InfMon2 TdaP 130 112 18 WHOLE_WT_IgG (16 NA); PT_ADCD (2 NA)

Step1 net: FHA FHA @ InfMon2 TdaP 130 112 18 WHOLE_WT_IgG (16 NA); FHA_ADCD (2 NA)
Step1 net: FHA FHA @ InfMon2 TT 134 109 25 WHOLE_WT_IgG (25 NA)
Step1 net: PRN PRN @ InfMon2 TdaP 130 112 18 WHOLE_WT_IgG (16 NA); PRN_ADCD (2 NA)
Step1 net: PRN PRN @ InfMon2 TT 134 109 25 WHOLE_WT_IgG (25 NA)
Step3 mediation [raw]: PT PT TdaP 130 128 2 PT_ADCD (2 NA)
Step3 mediation [raw]: PT PT TT 134 134 0 —
Step3 mediation [raw]: FHA FHA TdaP 130 128 2 FHA_ADCD (2 NA)
Step3 mediation [raw]: FHA FHA TT 134 134 0 —
Step3 mediation [raw]: PRN PRN TdaP 130 128 2 PRN_ADCD (2 NA)
Step3 mediation [raw]: PRN PRN TT 134 134 0 —

Step3 mediation [igg_std]: PT PT TT 134 134 0 —

Appendix — Reproducibility

# one endpoint, fast pass (skip the expensive NCT)
rmarkdown::render("pathway_analysis.Rmd",
                  params = list(endpoint = "SBA", run_nct = FALSE),
                  output_file = "pathway_SBA.html")

# full run
rmarkdown::render("pathway_analysis.Rmd",
                  params = list(endpoint = "SBA"),
                  output_file = "pathway_SBA.html")

Step 3 needs lavaan; step 5 uses car if present (falls back to a manual VIF otherwise); step 4 uses relaimpo for unique-variance shares; steps 1 & 6 need bootnet/qgraph/networktools/NetworkComparisonTest. Any missing package skips only its step, with an inline note. Place the six 03_step* children in parts/ and this file at the project root beside concurrent_analysis.Rmd.
